title: Myofibroblast Markers and Microscopy Detection Methods in Cell Culture and Histology
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"r7qeg","text":"The identification of myofibroblasts is essential for mechanistic in vitro studies, cell-based drug tests, and to assess the level of fibrosis in experimental animal or human fibrosis. The name myo-fibroblast was chosen in 1971 to express that the formation of contractile features-stress fibers is the essential criterion to define these cells. Additional neo-expression of α-smooth muscle actin (α-SMA) in stress fibers has become the most widely used molecular marker. Here, we briefly introduce the concept of different myofibroblast activation states, of which the highly contractile α-SMA-positive phenotype represents a most advanced functional stage. We provide targeted immunofluorescence protocols to assess this phenotype, and publicly accessible image analysis tools to quantify the level of myofibroblast activation in culture and in tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dsvru","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
